samples.xml_description:
<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulates 4 input data and outputs the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic for accumulator
        reg [9:0] accum;
        reg [1:0] counter;
        reg valid_out_reg;
        
        // Combinational logic for output assignment
        assign data_out = accum;
        assign valid_out = valid_out_reg;
        
        // Always block triggered on positive edge of clk or negative edge of rst_n
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition
                accum <= 10'd0;
                counter <= 2'd0;
                valid_out_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                // Check if valid_in is high
                if (valid_in == 1'b1) begin
                    // Add current data_in value to accum
                    accum <= accum + data_in;
                    
                    // Increment counter
                    counter <= counter + 1;
                    
                    // Check if counter reaches 3 (i.e., 4th input data)
                    if (counter == 2'd3) begin
                        // Set valid_out_reg to 1 to indicate completion of accumulation
                        valid_out_reg <= 1'b1;
                        
                        // Reset counter for next accumulation
                        counter <= 2'd0;
                    end else begin
                        // Reset valid_out_reg to 0 since not all 4 inputs have been processed yet
                        valid_out_reg <= 1'b0;
                    end
                end else begin
                    // Reset valid_out_reg if valid_in is low
                    valid_out_reg <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
